silos.concern: Content-inclusion projection (which content kinds/families/bodies appear in output). Two independent vocabularies for the same decision: an 8-boolean include_* dataclass with its own ContentKind enum, and the body_policy/exclude_block_kinds/include_roles fields on the intended algebra spec. Surfaces pick one or the other; formatting.format_session takes ContentProjectionSpec while the projection bridge takes ProjectionSpec.
silos.should_be: ProjectionSpec (BodyPolicy x EvidenceFamily x exclude_block_kinds/include_roles) in polylogue/surfaces/projection_spec.py is the single content-projection algebra; ContentProjectionSpec/ContentKind should be derived from or replaced by it.

silos.concern: Render-format token vocabulary. The set of legal output formats is declared in four unrelated places with visible drift ('text' vs 'plaintext'): the RenderFormat enum, a bare SESSION_OUTPUT_FORMATS tuple, a MACHINE_OUTPUT_FORMATS frozenset, and a per-view formats tuple repeated on every read-view profile.
implementations: polylogue/archive/viewport/profiles.py:88 (per-profile formats tuples; profiles.py:131 uses 'text' where SESSION_OUTPUT_FORMATS/RenderFormat use 'plaintext')
silos.should_be: RenderFormat enum in polylogue/surfaces/projection_spec.py owns the format vocabulary; view profiles, format normalizers, and CLI classifiers reference subsets of it instead of re-listing string literals.

silos.concern: Render destination + delivery. RenderSpec models destination as a RenderDestination enum with an .out path and a file-requires-path validator, but the CLI read-view stack carries destination as a bare str on ReadViewInvocation and reimplements file/clipboard/stdout dispatch in deliver_content().
silos.should_be: RenderSpec (RenderDestination + out) owns destination semantics and delivery; ReadViewInvocation should carry a RenderSpec, and deliver_content should be a single renderer-side sink keyed off RenderDestination.

silos.concern: Per-view flag clusters that re-declare projection/selection knobs the algebra already owns. Each read view has its own frozen options dataclass (limit/offset/full, edge_limit, neighbor limit/window_hours, related_limit, ...) even though ProjectionSpec already carries body_limit, body_offset, edge_limit, neighbor_limit, neighbor_window_hours.
silos.should_be: SelectionSpec + ProjectionSpec in projection_spec.py own these knobs; per-view option dataclasses collapse into projection/selection fields keyed by the view's projection family map (READ_VIEW_PROJECTION_FAMILIES).

silos.concern: Three independent session->HTML transforms. Export/read '--format html' renders through a Jinja template; block/projection rendering builds HTML from blocks in Python; the daemon web reader constructs session HTML as JS strings client-side. Same conceptual transform (session/message -> HTML) implemented three ways with three escaping/sanitizing regimes.
implementations: polylogue/daemon/web_shell.py:22 (inline <!DOCTYPE html> shell) + client-side JS HTML string construction (web_shell.py:573) across polylogue/daemon/web_shell_reader.py and siblings
silos.should_be: One session/message -> renderable -> HTML pipeline (blocks/html_messages feeding a single template renderer) that both the export path and the daemon web reader consume, instead of three parallel HTML emitters.

silos.concern: Status/readiness assembly implemented in three code paths. Direct-archive readiness, tier/ops/workload status, and compaction all live in a 2000-line CLI module; the daemon has its own status payload builders; and workload-diagnostics builds a third snapshot of overlapping archive-health fields.
silos.should_be: One status-assembly module producing a typed status/readiness payload that daemon status, CLI status, and workload diagnostics all read (per bead 703), rather than three overlapping assemblers.

silos.concern: Query params -> filter fields forked in the daemon HTTP fast path. build_query_spec_from_params (SessionQuerySpec.from_params) is the single params->spec lowerer, but the daemon split-archive fast path deliberately does NOT construct a SessionQuerySpec and instead re-derives every public structured filter (has_paste/has_tool_use/repo/has_type/tool/action.../cwd_prefix/title/min_messages...) from raw HTTP params in parallel, with an in-code comment that it 'must mirror those public params here'. The Lark DSL grammar itself is correctly the sole text->spec entrypoint across CLI/MCP/daemon/api; the fork is on the params->filter axis, not the DSL.
implementations: polylogue/daemon/http.py:1970 (split-archive fast path: 'does not construct a SessionQuerySpec directly, so it must mirror those public params here') through http.py:~2010 (_filter_kw assembly)
polylogue/daemon/http.py:984 (_query_params_from_http building SessionQuerySpec-compatible params by hand)
silos.should_be: SessionQuerySpec.from_params is the one params->spec algebra; the daemon fast path should build a spec (or reuse its lowered filter fields) rather than mirroring each public param, so a new filter field cannot silently exist in the spec builder but be dropped by the fast path.

proposed_beads.title: Route daemon split-archive fast path through SessionQuerySpec.from_params
proposed_beads.design: polylogue/daemon/http.py:1970 documents that the split-archive fast path intentionally does not construct a SessionQuerySpec and hand-mirrors every public structured filter (has_paste, has_tool_use, has_thinking, repo, has_type, tool/exclude_tool, action/exclude_action/action_sequence/action_text, referenced_path, cwd_prefix, title, min/max_messages, min/max_words, plus the shared _filter_kw block). This is a parallel implementation of build_query_spec_from_params (polylogue/archive/query/spec.py:498). A filter field added to the spec builder is silently absent from the daemon fast path until someone edits both sites. Collapse it: have the fast path build a SessionQuerySpec via from_params and read its lowered filter fields, keeping only the genuinely count/summary-specific plumbing (session_id passed separately) outside the spec. Prove parity with a test that enumerates SessionQuerySpec filter fields and asserts each is honored by the fast path.
proposed_beads.acceptance: The daemon split-archive list/search/count path derives all structured filters from a SessionQuerySpec built via from_params (no per-field re-read of HTTP params for filters the spec already models); a test enumerates SessionQuerySpec filter attributes and fails if the fast path drops any; the in-code 'must mirror those public params here' comment and its manual mirroring block are removed; render surfaces (openapi/cli-output-schemas) still verify.
